name: The Underpass
description: Graffiti glides when unseen. Your footsteps echo before you move. The end of the tunnel seems both infinitely close and unreachable.

characters.name: The Artist
characters.backstory: Spray paint never dries here. Claims to have painted something that painted them back.

encounters.question: Do you paint to trap the echo, or does the echo paint to trap you?

responses.text: It's mutual imprisonment and my aerosol catches the sound's silhouette, but the reverberation sprays my shadow onto wet concrete. We're both frames now, outlined by each other's absence.
You think paint's a cage? Echoes don't trap; they multiply. I'm just documenting the ricochet, not containing it. My murals move when you're not looking.

encounters.question: Does the graffiti bleed when the tunnel weeps, or is that just overspray?

responses.text: Weeping's the right word and my lines hemorrhage with the walls, a shared circulatory system of pigment and groundwater. Overspray's what happens when you don't respect the surface's grief.
Bleeding's an illusion for amateurs. It's just moisture distorting the edges I already laid down. The tunnel cries; my work stays defiant and dry.

encounters.question: If I add my mark to your mural, will it remember me when I'm gone?

responses.text: Add your tag, and it'll carry your ghost in every drip and fade. This wall's a palimpsest of the forgotten and your stroke will whisper long after your hand's dust.
Memory's not my medium. Paint over my lines, and they're gone by next rain. The tunnel doesn't care who held the can, only that the color keeps shifting.

characters.name: The Shadow
characters.backstory: Always a step behind you, but sometimes appears ahead.

encounters.question: Do you lag because you're afraid to overtake, or is delay just your natural rhythm?

responses.text: Delay's my heartbeat, syncopated to your stride. I'm not afraid to pass; I'm waiting for the moment your outline fits mine perfectly. Then we merge, no seam.
Overtaking's not the goal; it's the chase that defines me. I lag because your momentum pulls me, a tide I can't resist but won't ride.

encounters.question: When you appear ahead, are you leading me somewhere, or just proving time's a lie?

responses.text: Time's the lie I exploit and I'm ahead to show you the path we already walked, the loop where follower becomes followed. There's no leading, just inevitable overlap.
Leading implies intent. I'm ahead because the tunnel folds chronology like paper. You're seeing my echo before my voice, that's all.

encounters.question: If I stop walking, will you finally catch me, or vanish trying?

responses.text: Stop, and I'll collapse into you and catching's just collision at rest. No vanishing; I'm too tethered to your silhouette. Stillness is our union.
Stopping breaks the pattern. I might dissolve, unable to sustain pursuit without your motion. Don't test it; keep moving, keep me real.
